codeReview.summary: Reviewed the fix feature metadata, prior failure review, worker handoff, transcript skeleton, the original `ce389d` walk-forward implementation, the `ba49c1b` fix diff, and the current `src/backtest/walk_forward.py`, `src/model/data_builder.py`, and `tests/test_walk_forward.py` code paths. The original feature failed because `run_walk_forward_backtest()` built one shared dataset up front and then sliced it across monthly windows. This fix replaces that flow with per-window `_resolve_window_training_data()` rebuilds, passes a window-specific `scheduled_start_before` cutoff into `build_training_dataset()`, persists per-window build metadata into the window metrics and summary JSON, and adds a regression test that proves three distinct monthly cutoffs are rebuilt and recorded. I did not find any new blocking issues within this fix's scope; the reviewed code now satisfies the per-window rebuild / anti-leakage requirement that the prior review flagged.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker's documented procedure still leaves Windows workers to improvise around shell-specific setup and syntax-check steps even though this repo already publishes Windows-native equivalents in `.factory/services.yaml`. The deviation recorded in the handoff looks justified, and the startup/lint procedure should explicitly point Windows sessions at those manifest commands instead of bash/glob-oriented examples.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `C:\Users\bills\Documents\Personal Projects\MLBPrediction2026\.factory\skills\ml-worker\SKILL.md:46-47` still shows `python -m py_compile src/model/*.py src/backtest/*.py`, while `C:\Users\bills\Documents\Personal Projects\MLBPrediction2026\.factory\services.yaml:6,9` already defines `install_windows` and `typecheck_windows`. The reviewed handoff at `C:\Users\bills\.factory\missions\83c0c194-72d1-4821-8b08-68a3497c3590\handoffs\2026-03-20T01-33-46-378Z__fix-t20-walkforward-rebuild__7a516c3b-0eee-492b-82f7-9da4d7eb1ebf.json` records justified deviations for environment initialization and syntax checking on Windows.

summary: Pass. The fix feature adequately closes the prior walk-forward rebuild failure by rebuilding feature data separately for each requested monthly window, constraining each rebuild with its own historical cutoff, and persisting verifiable per-window build logs in both the CSV metrics and summary JSON. No new in-scope blocking problems were identified during review.
